meta.title: Should a mid-sized law firm replace 1Y associates with Claude + senior review?
meta.subtitle: Decision Brief — possibility map and recommendation

slides.title: Replace 1Y associates with Claude + senior review?
slides.subtitle: Decision Brief — possibility map and recommendation

slides.title: Full replacement is dominated; run a wrapped 18-month pilot with kill criteria, anchor clients, and Utah sandbox cover
slides.action: Do NOT eliminate the 1Y class. Cut the upcoming class 50-60% (4-5 instead of 8-12) + AI-augment with Claude + senior-review SOP + paid anchor-client advisory board (3-5 Fortune 500 GCs) + Utah Office of Legal Services Innovation sandbox + binding pre-committed kill criteria that auto-trigger a Cravath-scale rehire of the next class.

fields.label: CONFIDENCE
fields.value: MEDIUM-HIGH · converged at iteration 2 with unanimous rank-1 across pessimist, customer, regulator · regulator classifies CONDITIONALLY CLEAN

fields.label: DEPENDS ON
fields.value: Partners honor kill criteria when triggered [C2:assumption] · Anchor GCs accept paid advisory-board structure · Utah sandbox admits firm · Malpractice carrier non-opposition · Claude substitutability ~40% holds [C1:assumption]

fields.label: MONITOR
fields.value: Monthly: Claude substitutability audit · senior-review throughput · top-performer attrition · client complaint rate · Quarterly: OCI yield · summer-associate acceptance · Vault/Chambers commentary · peer firm AI ethics opinions

fields.label: PRE-MORTEM
fields.value: Partners treat kill criteria as aspirational (P=high) · Anchor co-design stalls past 6 months (P=0.35) · Hallucination incident in pilot year 1 (P=0.30) · Top performers defect within 6 months (P=0.45)

fields.label: DEEPEST DISAGREEMENT
fields.value: Regulator: H2 status quo wins on legal-cleanness (0.85 vs h6 0.75). Pessimist + others: h6 wins on strategic terms — reversibility + co-design + sandbox close the cleanness gap.

fields.label: REVIEW TRIGGER
fields.value: Re-run at month 6 (co-design outcome) · month 12 (substitutability data) · month 18 (kill criteria) · ABA revises Opinion 512 · State bar imposes AI-associate restrictions · Cravath-scale base moves >10%

slides.source: Source: Decision Brief (full mode, 2 iterations, unanimous iter-2 rank-1 on h6) · [G1], [G3], [C1:assumption], [C2:assumption]

slides.lead_question: A 5-persona council, 6 competing hypotheses, two iterations to convergence, 6 adversarial stress scenarios — what survived?

slides.title: The analysis explored 6 hypotheses across 2 iterations of a 5-persona council, stress-tested by 6 adversarial scenarios
bullets: 5 personas spawned as separate subagents — Optimist, Pessimist, Competitor, Regulator, Customer Advocate — each with independent context
6 hypotheses generated, ranging from full elimination (h1, adversary lethality 9/10) to wrapped 18-month pilot (h6 — synthesized from three independent iter-1 alternatives)
2 iterations — convergence reached at iteration 2 with 88% [C3:council] assumption stability and 0 unresolved contradictions
Adversary phase generated 6 scenarios: 3 worst-cases + 3 black swans (no irrational-actor cell on this brief)
Sensitivity analysis on 7 key assumptions — 3 high-sensitivity, 4 medium
Real-world anchors: ABA Formal Opinion 512 [G2] (six obligations), Mata v. Avianca [G4] sanctions baseline, Ropes & Gray TrAIlblazers [G7] precedent for structured AI integration without cuts
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief Convergence Log; engine-protocol.md

slides.title: Six hypotheses surfaced; only h6 (wrapped pilot) survived unanimous iter-2 rank-1 endorsement
rows: Claude 40%+ substitutability · Review batching works · Ranking damage reframable
Competitors slow-roll adoption · ABA 512 compliance manageable
Remaining 1Ys at 1.8-2x throughput · Retention bonuses feasible
Skip 1Y, hire at year-2 equivalent via laterals
Lateral premium 20-40% erases savings within 24-36 months
AI-ops talent pool stable · Bar scrutiny manageable · Caste risk
Wrapped 18-month pilot: h3 + co-design + sandbox + kill
Partners honor kill criteria · Anchor GCs accept advisory · Utah admits

slides.title: Plotted on reversibility × franchise risk, h6 sits in the manageable quadrant; h1 sits where every adversarial scenario amplifies

items.label: h1
items.legend: Full elimination — 7-yr pipeline rupture P=0.80, ranking re-rate 1-2 tiers, malpractice incident P=0.52

items.label: h4
items.legend: Lateral substitution — premium erases savings within 24-36 months (P=0.78)

items.label: h5
items.legend: Two-track JD — culture clash + bar supervision scrutiny on MRPC 5.3

items.label: h3
items.legend: Cut 50-60% standalone — efficiency without the wrapper, h6 minus safeguards

items.label: h2
items.legend: Status quo + Claude — regulator-cleanest path, no strategic upside

items.label: h6
items.legend: Wrapped 18-month pilot — kill criteria + anchor clients + sandbox preserve reversibility

slides.source: Source: Synthesis from Decision Brief — bubble size = council_agreement × probability of dominance

slides.lead_question: Where the personas converged, where they diverged, and what the disagreement itself reveals about the decision.

slides.title: The strongest signals point to pipeline rupture under elimination and structural antifragility under the wrapped pilot

effects.label: h1 partnership pipeline rupture (no 1Ys → no partners 2033)
h4 lateral premium erases savings within 24-36 months
Client AI fee scrutiny — GCs demand AI-disclosure covenants
h1 ranking damage — Vault drops 20-40 slots
Senior review overhead consumes 40-60% of h1 savings
Top 2Y-4Y performers defect within 6-9 months of h1
h3 remaining 1Y burnout (25-40% leave within 24 months)
h1 hallucination incident reaches court / client deliverable
Utah sandbox is the correct regulatory cover (not Arizona)

commentary_lines: Top 12 effects ranked by council_agreement × probability
Bar color = council agreement (navy = 5/5, fading to pale = 1/5)
Black bracket = persona disagreement range — width is the uncertainty signal
Two highest-probability 5/5 signals (pipeline rupture + lateral premium) both reject h1 elimination from independent angles
Regulator's Utah sandbox finding (1/5, P=0.90) — single-persona but load-bearing for h6 reversibility
slides.source: Source: Decision Brief — Effects Map (top 12 by council_agreement × probability) · [C4:pessimist], [C5:regulator], [C6:customer]

slides.title: The deepest disagreement — legal cleanness vs strategic upside — IS the data
left_items: Surfaced the hallucination-incident probability anchor (P=0.52 for h1) and the partnership pipeline rupture math (P=0.80) that broke h1
Designed the reversibility structure — kill criteria + binding triggers — that became the core of h6
Identified senior-review overhead as a 40-60% drag on h1's gross savings, eroding the cost rationale even before risk adjustment
Pessimist's iter-2 framing positioned h6 as 'h3 with the wrapper' — making the safeguard layers explicit and individually testable
right_items: Regulator: h2 (status quo + Claude) scores 0.85 [C7:regulator] on legal cleanness; h6 scores 0.75
Pessimist + Customer: h6's strategic upside dominates the 10-point cleanness gap — h2 forfeits the trust differentiator and category-leadership narrative
Resolution: h6 with the four regulator corrections (Utah-only, WARN-compliant, MRPC 1.5/1.6 covenants, pilot-disclosed offer letters) closes most of the gap
Uncertainty hotspot: Claude substitutability — persona range 25-60%, adversary flagged 40% as likely overstated; this is what the 90-day pilot exists to measure
BLIND SPOT caught: legal-ops paralegals are MORE substitutable than 1Ys (70%+ overlap) — they bear the heaviest cuts under h1 unless an AI-ops ladder exists

slides.title: Pessimist scored highest on adversarial coverage and structural depth; Optimist's narrow ranges drove its lowest peer rank
commentary_lines: Each persona scored 1-5 across five rigor dimensions, derived from peer-review and critique outputs
Pessimist's structural depth surfaced both the kill-criteria pilot and the senior-review overhead math — the two pillars of h6
Customer caught the blind spot — paralegals are more substitutable than 1Ys, plus the anchor-client co-design structure
Regulator's quantitative rigor on jurisdictional specificity (Utah vs Arizona) was unique — the regulatory cover is jurisdiction-specific, not generic
Optimist's narrow ranges and weak counter-factual depth flagged in critique — substitutability optimism not anchored in empirical pilot data
slides.source: Source: Synthesized from Decision Brief — Council Dynamics + Critique outputs

slides.title: Six adversarial scenarios — h6's wrapper defuses 4 of them; 2 remain (carrier non-renewal, Cravath-scale bump)

items: Hallucination incident in pilot year 1 — triggers kill criterion, loses 1-2 anchor clients, malpractice premium +15-40% (P=0.30)
Two top performers defect within 6 months of pilot announcement — institutional knowledge loss, vote-of-no-confidence read (P=0.45)
Anchor GC co-design stalls past 6 months — firm announces pilot without trust differentiator, signals uncertainty (P=0.35)
State bar disciplinary action against peer firm for AI-related MRPC violation — chills pilot, forces premature rollback (P=0.20)
Cravath-scale bump to $250-275K during pilot — cost rationale weakens if base salaries rise faster than Claude capability (P=0.25)
Malpractice carrier non-renewal of coverage for AI-augmented firms — existential, forces fallback to h2 immediately (P=0.10)
h6 SOP discipline cuts hallucination P from 0.52 (h1 pure) to 0.30 — review batching + 50% fewer Claude-handled matters
Utah sandbox provides partial regulatory cover for peer-firm-discipline contagion
Confirm carrier buy-in BEFORE pilot launch (Month 1 dependency) — converts black swan into bounded gate
Net savings computed in absolute dollars, not share-of-comp — Cravath bump doesn't invalidate pilot economics

slides.title: Three high-sensitivity assumptions are load-bearing — if any fails, the pilot collapses to unwrapped h3 with all of h1's downside

assumptions.label: Claude substitutes ~40% of 1Y hours with senior review
Partners honor pre-committed kill criteria when triggered
Lateral market for year 2-3 associates remains tight to 2029
Anchor clients accept paid advisory-board structure
Malpractice carriers underwrite AI usage in pricing
Utah sandbox admits mid-size firm with non-Utah clients
Vault / Chambers rankings respond to abolished 1Y class

commentary_lines: Bar length = sensitivity weight × number of effects impacted
Dark-blue bar = HIGH sensitivity, lighter = MEDIUM
Substitutability is the single empirical question the 90-day pilot exists to answer — pre-pilot persona range 25-60%, adversary flagged 40% as likely overstated
Kill-criteria honoring is the one assumption the firm controls directly — make it externally enforceable (board resolution, client-notified commitments, month-12 external audit)
Lateral market tightness is SOLID per Bloomberg/Biglaw Investor data — the only HIGH-sensitivity assumption with strong external support

slides.lead_question: Ten gates from week 1 to month 24 — every commitment is bounded by carrier, client, regulator, and pilot data.

slides.title: The recommendation maps to a ten-gate roadmap with explicit kill criteria at every stage
slides.subtitle: Every gate has a binding kill criterion; failure at any gate triggers pause, redesign, or full Cravath-scale rehire of next 1Y class
rows: Partner vote on binding kill criteria
Any kill criterion → revert to h2
If adopting: expand to other practice groups

slides.title: Selected sources
slides.subtitle: Tags appear inline throughout the deck — [G#] = ground data, [C#:persona] = council finding, [C#:assumption] = sensitivity assumption
rows: Fully loaded 1Y cost $400-450K/yr; class of 8-12 → $3.2-5.4M/yr gross savings
ABA Formal Opinion 512 (July 2024): six obligations on AI use
Cravath-scale 1Y base $225K; ~32% of firms match fully
Mata v. Avianca (2023): $5K sanctions baseline for hallucinated citations
26% of legal organizations using GenAI 2026 (up from 14% in 2024)
Malpractice carriers price AI: governed lowers premium, ungoverned +15-40%
Ropes & Gray TrAIlblazers: 380-400hr/yr AI skill-building credit for 1Ys
Claude substitutes ~40% of 1Y hours (range 25-60%, adversary flag)
Partners honor pre-committed kill criteria — SHAKEABLE
Utah Office of Legal Services Innovation = correct regulatory cover
h2 cleanness 0.85 vs h6 0.75 — narrow gap closed by 4 corrections
Hallucination incident pilot year 1 P=0.30 (down from 0.52 h1)
WARN Act 60-day notice if 1Y cuts trigger; MRPC 1.5/1.6 covenants
